name: AlphaX POS Terminal

fields.label: POS Outlet
fields.description: The business unit this terminal belongs to. Branch is derived from the outlet.

fields.label: Branch
fields.description: Auto-fetched from the outlet. Read-only: change the outlet's branch instead of editing here directly.

fields.label: PC Identity
fields.description: Captured during PC binding. Lets you see at a glance which physical machine corresponds to which terminal record. The bridge daemon (when installed) auto-populates these. Without the bridge, a manager can fill them in by hand during station setup.

fields.label: PC Hostname
fields.description: e.g. 'WIN-A4F2-COFFEE01'. Visible to admins for troubleshooting (not used for security).

fields.label: PC MAC Address
fields.description: Captured by the AlphaX bridge daemon if installed. Optional; useful for inventory tracking.

fields.label: PC UUID
fields.description: Hardware UUID captured by the bridge. Most stable identifier across OS reinstalls.

fields.label: Last Bound At
fields.description: When this terminal was last bound to a PC.

fields.label: Last Bound By
fields.description: The manager who last performed the binding.

fields.label: Default POS Profile
fields.description: If only one profile is allowed at this terminal, this is preselected. If multiple are allowed (see table below), this is the default for the picker.

fields.label: Allowed POS Profiles
fields.description: Which POS Profiles can be used at this terminal. Leave empty to allow any profile (legacy behavior; not recommended for new setups). When the cashier loads, the active profile is the intersection of this list with the user's allowed list.

description: Cashier station within an outlet. One physical PC binds to one terminal. The terminal record carries PC identity, the outlet/branch context, and which POS profiles can be used here.
